meta_title: For Freelancers: portfolio building to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Portfolio Building for Freelancers: Solving Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Learn how to overcome debugging hurdles by leveraging portfolio building with Antigravity and Claude Code. A high-efficiency guide for modern freelance developers.
content.answer_block: Stop struggling with opaque errors by shifting your workflow to portfolio building. By using Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for autonomous debugging, you transform complex bugs into modular, reusable components. This approach turns every technical challenge into a high-value asset for your professional developer portfolio.
content.intro:
The screen flickers with a cryptic stack trace that defies explanation. You’ve spent three hours chasing a ghost in the machine, and your client’s deadline is looming like a storm cloud. This is the silent killer of freelance momentum: the 'debug trap' where time spent equals zero revenue.

But what if you stopped viewing bugs as roadblocks and started seeing them as architectural opportunities? By treating every fix as a component for a portfolio piece, you flip the script from reactive troubleshooting to proactive product building. With the right stack—Antigravity and Claude Code—you can automate the heavy lifting and turn your codebase into a showcase of resilient, modular engineering.

steps.title: Define the Architectural Scope
steps.description: Before fixing, map out the component to ensure it is modular enough for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a modular React component structure for a [Feature Name] that handles error states gracefully and follows clean architecture principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project directory structure that isolates the buggy logic into a testable, standalone module.'

steps.title: Isolate the Buggy Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to extract the problematic function into a pure, testable unit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this code block and refactor it into a pure function that is decoupled from the main application state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Identify the specific side-effect causing the crash and move the logic into a separate file for isolated testing.'

steps.title: Implement Unit Regression Tests
steps.description: Create a suite of tests that proves the fix works, adding value to your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive Jest test suite that covers edge cases for this specific function, ensuring 100% branch coverage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate and execute unit tests for the extracted module to verify the fix works under various input parameters.'

steps.title: Document for Portfolio Value
steps.description: Add JSDoc and README files that explain the 'Why' behind your engineering choices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional technical README section explaining the complexity of this bug and the elegant pattern used to resolve it.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a markdown documentation file that describes the architectural pattern used to solve this bug for a portfolio case study.'

steps.title: Final Integration and Polish
steps.description: Re-integrate the clean component back into the main codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the integrated module for performance bottlenecks and suggest any final optimizations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate the tested module back into the main app and perform a final build check to ensure no regressions.'

pro_tips: Treat every bug fix as a 'mini-library'—if you solve it once, package it as an NPM module for your portfolio.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to maintain context across massive, legacy codebases that lack documentation.
Always commit your 'before' and 'after' code in separate branches to create a compelling 'GitHub contribution graph' story.
Focus on 'Error Handling as a Feature'—clients love developers who build systems that fail gracefully.
Automate your portfolio updates by pushing your documented fixes directly to a dedicated 'Engineering Case Studies' repository.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged and documented your first module, publish a short blog post on Dev.to or LinkedIn detailing the architectural challenge you solved to build your authority.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs drain your time and start turning your technical expertise into a high-converting asset. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
